notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 1 location for El Cerrito -- Castro Park -- with 6 outdoor hard courts, dedicated permanent lines and nets, restrooms and water, free/reservable access. Oddly, Pickleheads publishes this under two separate detail-page slugs with identical facts (/el-cerrito/castro-park and /el-cerrito/castro-park-pickleball-courts), which looks like an internal duplicate rather than two venues. El Cerrito's own official parks & recreation site (elcerrito.gov, the primary source per this site's standard) independently and explicitly confirms pickleball exists ONLY at Castro Park: its 'Courts & Play Fields' and 'Parks & Facilities' pages both list tennis courts at five other parks (Arlington, Canyon Trail, Harding, Tassajara, Cerrito Vista) but pickleball at Castro Park alone. This matters because several third-party SEO/lesson-marketing pages (e.g. carmensanzpickleball.com) vaguely describe 'pickleball courts' at Harding Park, Cerrito Vista Park, and the 'El Cerrito Community Center' using generic marketing language ('some of the best courts', 'top-notch facilities', 'favorite among local enthusiasts') with no court counts, addresses, or specifics -- none of this could be corroborated by the city's own site (which places only tennis, not pickleball, at those parks, and confirms the Community Center is a rental hall/Recreation Dept office with no gym or courts) or by any other independent source checked. Treated as unconfirmed padding and excluded, consistent with this site's no-padding standard. Also explicitly checked and excluded: 'iTennis Cerritos' / 'iPickle Cerritos' results, which are a same-name-collision false positive for Cerritos, CA (Los Angeles County, near Long Beach) -- a completely different city from El Cerrito, CA (Contra Costa County, East Bay) -- surfaced repeatedly by search due to the near-identical name; and Swimply's 'pickleball courts/clubs in El Cerrito' category pages, which are speculative marketplace SEO pages for a peer-to-peer backyard-court/pool rental platform with no specific address-verified active listing found for El Cerrito. Net result: exactly one confirmed real public pickleball venue in El Cerrito.

venues.name: Castro Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- 6 dedicated pickleball courts, converted from 2 former tennis courts in a 2025 renovation. Each court is individually fenced with an 8-foot perimeter buffer (a design response to the discovery that the original tennis courts were narrower than standard), with permanent lines.
venues.netType: Dedicated permanent pickleball nets, installed in the 2025 renovation ('permanent nets eliminate setup requirements' per the local news account of the project). A few aggregator listings not updated since the renovation still describe portable nets on lined tennis courts -- that reflects the pre-2025 state, not the current one.
venues.price: Free during scheduled open-play hours (minimum 16 hours/week guaranteed). Outside open-play hours, reservations are $8.25/hr for El Cerrito residents, $10.50/hr for non-residents, $21/hr for instructors (one-hour minimum; $1/hr fee for cancellations inside 48 hours), per the current elcerrito.gov Courts & Play Fields page. Several sources describing the July 2025 opening cite a flat introductory $8/$10 resident/non-resident rate, which appears to have been the rate at launch before a small increase to $8.25/$10.50.
venues.hours: Free open play: Sat-Sun 9am-12pm, Mon-Tue 10am-12pm, Wed-Fri 4-6pm (summer) / 3-5pm (winter) -- reservations are not available during these blocks. Reservable 8am-sundown outside open-play blocks; not lit for night play. Park grounds open sunrise to 1 hour after sunset daily.
amenities: Restrooms (key rental with refundable deposit at El Cerrito Community Center)
venues.skill: All levels welcome; each court is individually color-coded by skill level (green = beginner-friendly, blue = intermediate, red = advanced), per the local news account of the 2025 renovation.
venues.weather: East Bay bayside flatland climate immediately north of Berkeley/Albany -- cool, breezy, and prone to summer marine-layer fog and onshore wind off San Francisco Bay, especially mornings and evenings. El Cerrito's hillside (eastern) neighborhoods sit above some of the fog that blankets the flats, but Castro Park itself is on the flatland side near the BART corridor, so it gets the fuller onshore-wind/overcast pattern; play typically warms and clears by midday even in summer.
venues.notes: Address conflict: elcerrito.gov itself (two separate official pages) plus Yelp, PicklePlay/UTR Sports, CourtSource, mypacer.com, and Roadtrippers all state 1420 Norvell St, El Cerrito, CA 94530. A smaller cluster -- DailyPB, Pickleballify, RIPL Pickleball, and Bounce.game -- instead states 1441 Lawrence St, El Cerrito. Lawrence St is a real El Cerrito street, but no address record for a '1441 Lawrence St' turned up in a direct check, while Norvell St's numbering and the venue match cleanly; treating the official city address (1420 Norvell St) as correct and the Lawrence St figure as a likely copied error circulating among a subset of aggregators. Not treated as a blocking conflict since it doesn't affect the two core facts (court count, indoor/outdoor). History: this location held 2 tennis courts until they were converted into 6 dedicated pickleball courts, which opened Tuesday, July 8, 2025 (funded by $100,437 raised by the East Bay Pickleball Association plus $29,000 from the city's Castro maintenance budget), with a grand-opening event July 16, 2025; a Phase 2 (seating, shade, bike storage, improved disabled access) was under consideration per the same article but not confirmed built. One aggregator (pickleballrush.com) still shows the stale pre-renovation '2 courts / tennis courts' listing -- superseded by every source checked that reflects the 2025 renovation. lat/lon were derived by geocoding the confirmed street address via two independent third-party map aggregators (mypacer.com, Roadtrippers), which converged within roughly 30 meters of each other; this is not an official city GIS coordinate or a directly-confirmed Google Maps pin, so approxLocation is set true per this repo's convention even though the address itself is well-confirmed. No numeric Google Maps cid could be found via search (a direct fetch of maps.google.com for this venue returned no usable identifier), so googleMapsUrl is left null rather than substituting a place_id or /maps/place/ URL. Restroom access requires a key, rented with a refundable deposit at the El Cerrito Community Center -- not simply open to walk-up use, worth flagging for visiting players. Pickleheads itself shows no visible star rating for this venue (0 reviews per Bounce.game's independent listing); the 4.3 rating found elsewhere belongs to PicklePlay's own rating system, not Pickleheads, so pickleheadsRating is left null rather than borrowed from a different platform.
